Title: Ethylene mediates the branching of the jasmonate-induced flavonoid biosynthesis pathway by suppressing anthocyanin biosynthesis in red Chinese pear fruits

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Jasmonate signaling is proposed to induce degradation of PpJAZ proteins, releasing TFs that promote anthocyanin or flavone/isoflavone biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signaling control of flavonoid biosynthesis branch selection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome analysis and model inference from hormone treatments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ proteins were proposed as jasmonate signaling repressors in pear fruit coloration. 2. In the absence of ethylene, jasmonate-induced JAZ degradation releases TFs such as MYB and bHLH to activate flavonoid biosynthesis. 3. This role was proposed in the model rather than directly functionally validated in pear in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is central to the mechanistic model of hormone-controlled flavonoid branching, but direct pear-specific functional assays were not reported.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point downstream of flavanone precursor utilization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of flavone/isoflavone branch products from flavanone precursors.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms branch products leading to deep yellow coloration-associated flavones/isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavone/isoflavone biosynthesis associated with deep yellow coloration under MeJA and ethylene-responsive conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively responsive to ethylene and jasmonate; positively correlated with yellow coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/PacBio transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpFNS and PpIFS genes were predominantly assigned to candidate set 4. 2. Their expression was positively responsive to both ethylene and jasmonate. 3. Their induction was associated with flavone/isoflavone accumulation and deep yellow fruit coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses PpFNS and PpIFS together and does not fully separate individual family members or accession IDs in the main text.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of anthocyanin pigments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration; ethylene suppresses this anthocyanin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with anthocyanin biosynthesis; suppressed under ethylene-responsive conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/PacBio transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; anthocyanin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpANS expression pattern matched anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Ethylene inhibited anthocyanin biosynthesis partly by blocking PpANS expression. 3. In the absence of ethylene, jasmonate promoted anthocyanin biosynthesis with higher late biosynthetic gene expression.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Late biosynthetic gene; terminal stabilization step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration; ethylene represses its expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PpUFGT2 showed similar expression pattern to PpMYB10 and PpMYB114 and was positively correlated with anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/PacBio transcriptomics; co-expression analysis; anthocyanin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpUFGT expression was positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Ethylene blocked expression of PpUFGT genes. 3. Elevated expression under 1-MCP or MeJA+1-MCP accompanied red coloration.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation step feeding anthocyanin and flavone/isoflavone precursor diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces hydroxyl groups into flavonoid intermediates affecting branch substrate composition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies modified precursors to anthocyanin branch and affects competition with other branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene down-regulation of these genes leads to flavanone accumulation, favoring precursor availability for flavone/isoflavone biosynthesis over anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ethylene down-regulated PpF3'H and PpF3'5'H expression. 2. This was associated with hyper-accumulation of flavanones. 3. The resulting precursor pool likely contributed to flavone/isoflavone production under ethylene presence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as pathway-branch determining enzymes rather than individually validated genes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; suppressed by ethylene via upstream TF repressors
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpANS; PpUFGT and other late biosynthetic genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration; ethylene suppresses its expression, reducing anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in red pear fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hormone treatments; transcriptome profiling; co-expression network analysis; qRT-PCR support
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation; negatively responsive to ethylene; induced by MeJA in absence of ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB10 expression was positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration. 2. Ethylene suppressed PpMYB10 expression, thereby down-regulating anthocyanin late biosynthetic genes. 3. Jasmonate induced PpMYB10 expression when ethylene was blocked, promoting red coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing red coloration and anthocyanin accumulation in pear, especially combined with strategies reducing ethylene inhibition.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; repressed by ethylene, potentially via ERF repressors
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpANS; PpUFGT and other anthocyanin pathway genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration; ethylene suppresses expression and thereby represses anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of red pear anthocyanin accumulation; specifically highlighted as central ethylene target
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hormone treatments; transcriptome profiling; co-expression network analysis; promoter motif analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation; negatively responsive to ethylene; induced by MeJA in the absence of ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB114 was one of the key regulatory genes positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Ethylene suppressed PpMYB114 expression. 3. ERF-binding sites were found in the PpMYB114 promoter, supporting indirect ethylene-mediated transcriptional repression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering red-skinned pear cultivars with elevated anthocyanin content by avoiding ethylene-mediated repression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ethylene signal transduction; indirect suppression of anthocyanin regulators through downstream TF repressors
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hormone-mediated branch regulation of flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Proposed indirect effect on PpMYB10 and PpMYB114 via ERF/MYB/bHLH repressors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ethylene-responsive repression of anthocyanin regulatory genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Subject to negative feedback through PpEBF1 genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in ethylene signaling that suppresses anthocyanin accumulation and favors branching toward flavone/isoflavone accumulation under jasmonate-plus-ethylene conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central ethylene signaling hub proposed to mediate ethylene dominance over jasmonate in pathway branching
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling; hormone response analysis; mechanistic inference
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most PpEIN3/EIL genes increased in response to ethylene and jasmonate
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Most PpEIN3/EIL genes were induced by ethylene and jasmonate. 2. The data suggest jasmonate positively regulates the ethylene signaling pathway in pear. 3. EIN3/EIL was proposed to indirectly inhibit PpMYB10 and PpMYB114 through other TF repressors.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Indirect regulatory role inferred; direct binding to pear target promoters was not tested in this study.
